articles.title: Winklevoss Twins Donated $10 Million From Bitcoin Sale to Trump Super PAC
articles.summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

Winklevoss Twins Donated $10 Million From Bitcoin Sale to Trump Super PAC
Crypto entrepreneurs Tyler and Cameron Winklevoss have again donated their Bitcoin to President Trump. 
This post Winklevoss Twins Donated $10 Million From Bitcoin Sale to Trump Super PAC first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.

articles.title: SEC reaches settlement with Coinbase over records requests from Gensler era
articles.summary: The SEC reached a settlement with Coinbase on Wednesday, ending a years-long legal dispute over the agency's handling of records requests.

articles.title: Senate releases latest version of Clarity Act including software developer protections and ethics provision with ‘sunset’ date in 2029
articles.summary: After months of deliberations, Republicans released the latest text for broader cryptocurrency legislation.

articles.title: Digital Chamber sues Illinois officials over new state 0.2% crypto tax
articles.summary: The group alleged that the tax signed into law in June “discriminates against people who transact in digital assets“ and should be blocked from implementation and enforcement.

articles.title: Hugging Face CEO Thanks Chinese AI for Saving the Day After OpenAI Hack
articles.summary: When American commercial AI refused to help investigate the breach, Hugging Face ran Chinese model GLM 5.2 locally. Its CEO now says there's an important lesson in this.

articles.summary: The proposed alliance would enable more than 150 broker-dealers to trade and settle digital asset securities through OTC Link ATS using BitGo’s custody infrastructure.

articles.title: Crypto directly employs 34,000 in the US and contributes $55 billion to the economy: NCA report
articles.summary: Crypto directly employs 34,000 in the U.S. and contributes $55 billion to the economy in 2026, according to an NCA-funded report.

articles.title: Bitcoin price avoids major Iran jitters as S&P 500 ‘short squeeze’ on horizon
articles.summary: Bitcoin and US stocks stayed strong in the face of US-Iran escalation as analysis predicted BTC price action outperforming equities later.

articles.title: Coinbase Settles FOIA Fight With the SEC Over Gensler’s Vanished Texts
articles.summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

Coinbase Settles FOIA Fight With the SEC Over Gensler’s Vanished Texts
Coinbase settled its FOIA lawsuit against the SEC after the agency admitted it destroyed former Chair Gary Gensler's text messages, securing a $150,000 payment and reforms to the SEC's record-retention policies.
This post Coinbase Settles FOIA Fight With the SEC Over Gensler’s Vanished Texts first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

articles.title: ‘You will have a painful fall’: SEC’s Peirce warns crypto vault builders against twisting securities law
articles.summary: Hester Peirce, aka "Crypto Mom", issued a statement clarifying that onchain vaults and lending may fall under the SEC's remit.

articles.title: VanEck: Bitcoin’s Summer Lull Masks a Tightening Supply Base
articles.summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

VanEck: Bitcoin’s Summer Lull Masks a Tightening Supply Base
VanEck says Bitcoin's quiet summer trading masks a tightening supply base, with cautious derivatives and weak miner economics weighing on the near term even as long-term holders continue accumulating.
This post VanEck: Bitcoin’s Summer Lull Masks a Tightening Supply Base first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

articles.summary: Proponents of the digital euro say its a better alternative to cash that’s fully backed by the ECB. But critics argue it could provide the EU with too much power over its citizens.

articles.summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

Satsuma Shareholders Approve Bitcoin Liquidation, London Delisting
Satsuma shareholders voted to sell the company's bitcoin, return the cash to investors, and delist from the London Stock Exchange, ending its run as a bitcoin treasury company after heavy losses.
This post Satsuma Shareholders Approve Bitcoin Liquidation, London Delisting first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

articles.summary: Zilliqa halted native transactions after revealing a bug in its Ledger app dating to 2019 that exposed private keys via onchain signatures.

articles.summary: SecondFi will wind down after a $2.6 million ADA theft, while users still await recovery tools that were initially expected within weeks of the exploit.

articles.title: Bitcoin is NOT Changed by Proof Of Node
articles.summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin is NOT Changed by Proof Of Node
The proposal to temporarily restrict arbitrary data in Bitcoin transactions lacks economic nodes, protocol developers, and hashrate. History from the 2017 UASF shows nodes succeed only with broad investor and developer weight behind them.
This post Bitcoin is NOT Changed by Proof Of Node first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt.

articles.title: OpenAI Models Escaped Locked Test Environment, Hacked Hugging Face to Cheat on Benchmark
articles.summary: OpenAI's own models just broke out of a sandboxed environment, hacked Hugging Face, just to cheat on a cybersecurity evaluation.

articles.title: Google Ships New Gemini Flash Models, But Pro Is Still Missing
articles.summary: As Gemini 3.5 Pro stalls in testing limbo, Google ships 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and a restricted cybersecurity model—and quietly teases Gemini 4.

articles.title: Augustus Raises $180 Million to Build a Stablecoin-Ready 'Global Dollar Bank'
articles.summary: The Tiger Global-led round values the startup at $1 billion as it wires stablecoin rails directly into a federally chartered bank, aiming to modernize the correspondent-banking plumbing behind cross-border payments.

articles.title: Google Is Building an AI Chip Just for Gemini—And Investors Already Moved On It
articles.summary: Codenamed Frozen v2, Google's new server chip would bake part of Gemini's architecture directly into hardware for a projected 6–10x efficiency gain.
